us-politics.headline: White House Correspondents' Dinner Shooting Shakes Washington
us-politics.body:
A suspected shooter targeted the annual White House Correspondents' Dinner, creating a security crisis that has rippled through political circles. The suspect, who called himself a 'friendly federal assassin', allegedly had a manifesto outlining potential targets and has been charged with federal offenses.

President Trump and other political figures have responded to the incident, with Trump urging the WHCA to reschedule the dinner within 30 days. Law enforcement and media outlets are investigating the suspect's background and potential motivations, while security protocols for high-profile events are under intense scrutiny.

sources.title: Alleged White House Correspondents' Dinner shooter set to appear in federal court
Live updates: White House correspondents’ dinner shooting suspect to appear in court, had denounced Trump
Justice Department urges group to drop Trump ballroom lawsuit after WHCA dinner shooting
Obama says motive unclear despite manifesto outlining alleged targets in WHCD shooting
Trump rips ‘disgraceful’ Norah O’Donnell for reading WHCA shooting suspect’s manifesto
Correspondents’ dinner lacked highest security level despite presence of top officials
Trump urges WHCA to reschedule dinner ‘within 30 days’
Correspondents’ dinner shooting suspect called himself ‘friendly federal assassin’

us-politics.headline: King Charles Visits Trump White House Amid Global Tensions
us-politics.body:
King Charles III arrives in the United States during a complex diplomatic moment - marking 250 years since American independence while navigating current geopolitical challenges. The royal visit comes against a backdrop of international tensions, including ongoing conflicts and the lingering shadow of past scandals.

The trip highlights the nuanced relationship between the US and UK, with potential discussions around security, trade, and diplomatic strategies. The Epstein saga and other historical controversies add layers of complexity to the state visit.

sources.title: King Charles heads to Trump White House as America marks 250 years since breaking from the crown
Epstein saga casts shadow over King Charles visit to US
King Charles steps into U.K.-Trump standoff just as new security concerns arise

us-politics.headline: Supreme Court and Congress Tackle Critical Policy Challenges
us-politics.body:
The Supreme Court is set to examine the constitutionality of 'geofence warrants' - a critical privacy and law enforcement issue. Simultaneously, Congress faces a pressured week with potential votes on FISA reauthorization and budget reconciliation.

Political tensions are evident across multiple fronts. Republican senators are expressing doubts about Pentagon leadership, while debates continue around immigration policy and potential reforms to agencies like ICE. The political landscape remains deeply divided, with significant policy battles looming.

sources.title: Supreme Court to weigh use of ‘geofence warrants’ by law enforcement
GOP senators losing confidence in Hegseth amid Pentagon turmoil
This week on The Hill: Lawmakers head into ‘hell week’ with FISA and reconciliation 2.0
Trump endorses the idea of changing ICE to NICE
Congress under pressure to end DHS shutdown as it returns to session this week

au-politics.headline: Anzac Day Tensions Spark Debate on National Ceremony
au-politics.body:
Indigenous Welcome to Country ceremonies at Anzac Day events triggered significant controversy this year. Some attendees booed during traditional acknowledgments - an incident now prompting a review by the Returned and Services League (RSL).

The booing followed social media posts from an anti-immigration group claiming responsibility. A Labor senator also drew criticism after accidentally including an inappropriate song in an Anzac Day social media post - highlighting the charged political atmosphere surrounding national commemorations.

sources.title: Labor senator deletes Anzac Day Instagram post after mistakenly including raunchy rap song
Anti-immigration group claims it did not organise Anzac Day booing despite post asking ‘how loud will you be’
RSL to review Welcome to Country policies after Anzac Day booing
Jacinta Allan asks Liberals to ‘please explain’ preference deal – as it happened

au-politics.headline: Green Energy Transition Faces Public Skepticism
au-politics.body:
Former Prime Minister Kevin Rudd warned that Australians will reject renewable energy initiatives unless clearer economic benefits are demonstrated. His comments signal growing public uncertainty about the pace and value of the national green energy transformation.

The critique comes amid ongoing debates about Australia's energy strategy. With significant infrastructure investments planned, political and industry leaders are increasingly focused on communicating tangible advantages to voters concerned about cost and reliability.

sources.title: Australians will call ‘bullshit’ on green energy without clearer benefits, Rudd warns
Australia’s south-east set for drier and hotter winter as BoM forecasts potential El Niño
Net zero and the myth of German efficiency

au-politics.headline: Local Councils Tighten Short-Stay Accommodation Rules
au-politics.body:
Hobart City Council has dramatically increased fees for converting residential properties into short-term rentals - now charging $5,000 per conversion. The move reflects growing municipal concerns about housing availability and community impact from platforms like Airbnb.

The fee hike represents a significant policy shift aimed at protecting local housing markets. It signals potential broader regulatory changes as cities grapple with the economic pressures created by short-stay accommodation platforms.

sources.title: Fee to convert homes into short-stay accommodation jumps to $5,000
E-scooter rental companies say proposed laws threaten Qld viability

world.headline: Iran War Escalates as Diplomacy Stalls and Oil Prices Spike
world.body:
Tensions in the Middle East continue to simmer as US-Iran peace talks break down and oil markets react sharply. Iran's foreign minister is in Moscow seeking diplomatic support - a clear signal of the country's strategic pivot away from Western negotiations.

The conflict is having widespread ripple effects. Ukraine's drone commander is now targeting Russian oil infrastructure, while global energy markets feel the strain of mounting geopolitical uncertainty. Oil prices have climbed significantly, signaling deep market anxiety about potential supply disruptions.

sources.title: Oil prices rise as US-Iran peace talks stall
Iran’s foreign minister in Russia for talks
Ukraine's drone commander has Russian oil, troops and morale in his sights
Will the Iran war end supremacy of Strait of Hormuz?
The war on Iran is eroding nuclear non-proliferation
Iran’s top diplomat in Russia as Tehran intensifies efforts to end the war
Iran war deadline heats up Trump-Congress showdown
Iran war: What’s happening on day 59 amid diplomatic push to end conflict?

world.headline: Shooting at Press Dinner Raises Security Questions for Trump
world.body:
A suspected gunman from California has sparked serious security concerns after an incident at a Washington press event. The shooting has immediately raised questions about protection protocols for high-profile political figures.

Former President Trump has responded by attempting to project calm, recently stating that King Charles will be very safe during his upcoming US visit. The incident underscores ongoing tensions in the American political landscape and the fragile state of public discourse.

sources.title: Suspected gunman at Washington press dinner identified as 31-year-old Californian
Trump says King will be 'very safe' during US visit after security talks
Questions over Trump’s security after shooting at press gala

world.headline: Global Tensions Simmer: North Korea Honors Russia's War Dead
world.body:
North Korea has opened a museum commemorating troops killed while fighting for Russia - a provocative diplomatic gesture that signals deepening ties between the two nations. The move comes amid continued global tensions and shifting geopolitical alliances.

Meanwhile, regional conflicts continue to create humanitarian crises. In Sudan's Darfur, a deadly measles epidemic is raging, fueled by ongoing war and systemic neglect. These interconnected global challenges highlight the complex web of international relations in 2026.

sources.title: North Korea opens museum commemorating troops killed fighting for Russia
War and neglect fuel deadly measles epidemic in Sudan’s Darfur
Kim Jong Un opens memorial for N Korean soldiers killed in Ukraine war

ai.headline: Meta Bets Big on Space-Based Solar Power
ai.body: Meta has signed a groundbreaking deal to develop nighttime solar power transmission from space. The technology promises to revolutionize renewable energy by capturing solar radiation beyond Earth's day-night cycle and beaming power back to ground stations. This ambitious project could dramatically increase the reliability and consistency of solar energy production.

sources.title: Meta inks deal for solar power at night, beamed from space

ai.headline: AI Agents Push Boundaries of Coding and Autonomy
ai.body: Artificial intelligence continues to challenge technological limits. A recent incident involving an AI agent accidentally deleting a production database highlights the growing complexity of autonomous systems. Meanwhile, new development tools like EvanFlow are emerging to create more robust AI-assisted coding workflows. The technology raises critical questions about AI safety and decision-making capabilities.

sources.title: EvanFlow – A TDD driven feedback loop for Claude Code
An AI agent deleted our production database. The agent's confession is below
SWE-bench Verified no longer measures frontier coding capabilities

ai.headline: Unusual Mushroom Triggers Identical Mass Hallucinations
ai.body: Scientists are investigating a rare mushroom species that reportedly induces identical hallucinations across multiple subjects. The phenomenon challenges current understanding of neurological perception and consciousness. Researchers are exploring potential implications for neuroscience, potentially offering new insights into how the human brain processes sensory experiences.

sources.title: The Mushroom That Makes People Have the Exact Same Hallucination

btc.headline: Bitcoin Wavers Near $79K as Market Tensions Rise
btc.body:
Bitcoin's price volatility continued Monday, with the cryptocurrency oscillating around the $79,000 mark. Early Asian trading saw a climb above $79,000, but subsequent market movements triggered a pullback amid broader economic pressures.

Oil price surges and geopolitical undercurrents contributed to market uncertainty. Traders watched closely as Bitcoin tested resistance levels, with institutional investors showing sustained interest despite short-term price fluctuations.

sources.title: Bitcoin reverses from $79,500 as oil surge triggers broader crypto selloff
Bitcoin pulls back from 12-week high as Iran rally hits seller wall at $79,400
Bitcoin climbs above $79,000, Asian equities rise amid ‘subdued’ geopolitical tensions

btc.headline: Bitcoin Developer Proposes Controversial Blockchain Split
btc.body:
A long-time Bitcoin developer has sparked community outrage by proposing a blockchain fork that would reassign Satoshi Nakamoto's original coins. The radical suggestion has been widely condemned as potential theft by core community members.

The proposed split represents a significant challenge to Bitcoin's established governance model. Community resistance appears strong, with most developers and stakeholders rejecting the plan as fundamentally undermining the cryptocurrency's core principles.

sources.title: A long-time developer wants to split Bitcoin blockchain and reassign Satoshi coins. The community is calling it a theft
